name: Farida Bano
title: Zari & Textile Embroiderer
bio: Farida specialises in chikankari-inspired zari work for our embroidered textile jewellery line. She works from a sunlit room where silk is stretched on frames and the only sound is her needle passing through cloth — a rhythm she has kept for twenty-five years.
quote: Thread carries intention. I embroider as if the wearer is in the room.

name: Priya Sharma
title: Block Print Textile Artist
bio: Priya carves wooden blocks and prints natural dyes onto cotton and linen for our cloth earrings and fabric necklaces. Each motif is pressed by hand — no two prints are identical, and she considers that the soul of the piece.
quote: A print is a fingerprint. The block remembers every press.

name: Meenakshi Iyer
title: Silk Thread Weaver
bio: Meenakshi winds silk thread into gajras, tassels, and braided necklace strands. She learned from her grandmother how to match thread tension so each coil holds its shape through seasons of wear.
quote: Thread must hold memory — of the hand that wound it and the hair that wears it.

name: Ravi Desai
title: Upcycled Fabric Innovator
bio: Ravi transforms reclaimed denim, sari borders, and factory offcuts into cuffs and sustainable fashion accessories. His workshop is a library of rescued textiles — each scrap tagged with origin and story.
quote: Nothing is waste until we decide to throw it away. Fabric deserves second lives.

name: Kavita Patel
title: Mirror Work Specialist
bio: Kavita stitches shisha mirrors into fabric chokers and hair pins using techniques passed through her Kutchi community. Each mirror is set by hand so light catches at every angle of movement.
quote: Mirrors do not reflect vanity — they reflect the joy of the person wearing them.
